title: Classify Line from Standard Form
description: Determine whether a linear equation in standard form represents a vertical line, horizontal line, or neither.
condition: Linear equation in the form Ax + By = C with both A ≠ 0 and B ≠ 0.
response: State that the graph is a line that is neither vertical nor horizontal.
label: Principle / Model
example: For $5x - 2y = 3$, both coefficients are nonzero, so the graph is neither vertical nor horizontal.

title: Classify Linear Equation by Solution Type
description: Determine whether a linear equation has one solution, infinitely many solutions, or no solution based on its simplified form.
condition: A linear equation simplifies to either a true statement (e.g., 5 = 5), a false statement (e.g., 3.5 = -3.5), or an equation of the form x = a.
response: State whether the equation has infinitely many solutions, no solution, or one solution, respectively.
label: Principle / Model
example: If simplifying gives $3.5 = -3.5$, conclude there is **no solution**.

title: Classify Lines by Slope and Intercept
description: Determine whether two linear equations represent the same line, parallel lines, or neither by comparing slopes and y-intercepts.
condition: Given two linear equations (in any form) that can be written in slope-intercept form.
response: Rewrite both as y = mx + b; if slopes and intercepts match → same line; if slopes match but intercepts differ → parallel; otherwise → neither.
label: Principle
example: Compare $y=4x-\tfrac{5}{2}$ and $-8x+2y=5$. Rewrite second: $2y=8x+5 \Rightarrow y=4x+\tfrac{5}{2}$. Slopes equal (4) but intercepts differ, so the lines are parallel.

title: Clear Fraction by Multiplying Both Sides
description: Eliminate a fractional coefficient by multiplying both sides of an equation by a common denominator.
condition: Equation contains a fractional coefficient (e.g., 1/2(4x − 1) − 3 = ax + b).
response: Multiply both sides by the denominator to remove the fraction.
label: Rule / Plan

title: Combine Like Terms
description: Add or subtract coefficients of terms with the same variable and exponent.
condition: An expression containing like terms (e.g., $ax + bx$ or constants).
response: Add or subtract coefficients to produce a simplified term.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compare Slopes of Two Lines
description: Compare slopes numerically to determine which line is steeper or has greater slope.
condition: Two linear equations or representations with identifiable slopes.
response: Determine which slope is greater or which line is steeper.
label: Principle
example: If $m_d = 3$ and $m_n = \frac{5}{2}$, then $m_d > m_n$, so line $d$ is steeper.

title: Convert Repeating Decimal to Fraction
description: Rewrite a repeating decimal as a fraction using place value structure.
condition: Decimal has repeating block (e.g., 0.\overline{83}).
response: Write repeating digits over 99 (or appropriate number of 9s) and simplify.
label: Rule / Plan
notes: Assumes two-digit repeat block. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Convert Standard Form to Slope-Intercept Form
description: Rewrite a linear equation in standard form Ax + By = C into slope-intercept form y = mx + b.
condition: Equation in the form Ax + By = C.
response: Solve for y by isolating y to obtain y = mx + b.
label: Principle
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Determine One-Solution Condition for Linear Equation
description: Identify when a linear equation in x has exactly one solution based on coefficient comparison.
condition: Equation simplifies to form mx + k = ax + b.
response: State there is one solution if m ≠ a; no or infinite solutions if m = a depending on constants.
label: Principle / Model

title: Determine Unit Rate from Table
description: Find the constant of proportionality by dividing output values by corresponding input values in a proportional table.
condition: Given a proportional relationship table with corresponding (x, y) values.
response: Compute unit rate k = y / x for any pair.
label: Principle / Model

title: Distribute Multiplication Over Addition
description: Apply the distributive property to expand an expression.
condition: Expression of form k(a + b).
response: Rewrite as ka + kb.
label: Principle / Model

title: Distribute Rational Coefficient
description: Apply distributive property with fractional coefficients.
condition: Expression of form k(a + b) where k is rational.
response: Multiply k by each term inside parentheses.
label: Principle

title: Equal Distances for Same Arrival Time
description: Set distances equal when two travelers arrive at the same location at the same time.
condition: Two objects travel to the same destination and arrive simultaneously, possibly with different speeds and start times.
response: Create an equation equating their distances: rate × time expressions are equal.
label: Principle / Model
example: If train time is $t$, taxi time is $t - \tfrac{1}{5}$, so $35t = 50\left(t - \tfrac{1}{5}\right)$.

title: Evaluate Linear Expression by Substitution
description: Substitute given values for variables into a linear expression and simplify.
condition: Given a linear expression such as Ax + By and specific numerical values for x and y.
response: Compute the numerical value by multiplying and adding/subtracting terms.
label: Production / Skill

title: Generate Ordered Pairs from Linear Equation
description: Find ordered pairs that satisfy a linear equation by choosing a value for one variable and solving for the other.
condition: Given a linear equation in two variables and a selected value for one variable.
response: Solve for the other variable and state the ordered pair.
label: Principle / Model

title: Graph Line from Point and Slope
description: Graph a line by plotting a given point and using the slope as rise over run to find additional points.
condition: Given a slope m and a point (x, y) on the line.
response: Plot the point and use rise/run to locate another point, then draw the line through them.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Given slope -4 and point (-2,3): from (-2,3) move right 1 and down 4 to (-1,-1), then draw the line.

title: Graph Line from Standard Form
description: Rewrite standard form as slope-intercept form and graph using slope and intercept.
condition: Linear equation in standard form ax + by = c.
response: Solve for y, identify slope and intercept, and draw line.
label: Principle
example: From $3x+y=-2$, solve $y=-3x-2$ and graph with slope -3 and y-intercept -2.

title: Identify Graph of Linear Equation
description: Recognize that a first-degree equation in two variables graphs as a nonhorizontal line unless slope is zero.
condition: Equation in form ax + by = c with a and b not both zero.
response: State that its graph is a line (nonhorizontal if slope ≠ 0).
label: Concept

title: Identify Horizontal Line Equation
description: Recognize that an equation of the form y = constant represents a horizontal line.
condition: Equation written as y = k, where k is a constant.
response: State that the graph is a horizontal line at y = k.
label: Principle / Model
example: $y=5$ represents a horizontal line crossing the y-axis at 5.

title: Identify Repeating Decimal from Fraction
description: Determine that a fraction has a repeating decimal when its simplified denominator contains prime factors other than 2 or 5.
condition: A fraction in simplest form whose denominator has a prime factor other than 2 or 5.
response: Classify the decimal equivalent as repeating.
label: Principle / Model

title: Identify Slope from Graph
description: Determine the slope of a line by calculating rise over run from two points on its graph.
condition: A graphed straight line with at least two visible points.
response: Select two points and compute slope as change in y divided by change in x.
label: Principle
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Identify Terminating Decimal from Fraction
description: Determine that a fraction has a terminating decimal when its simplified denominator has only prime factors 2 and/or 5.
condition: A fraction in simplest form whose denominator’s prime factors are only 2 and/or 5.
response: Classify the decimal equivalent as terminating.
label: Principle / Model

title: Identify Vertical Line Equation
description: Recognize that an equation of the form x = constant represents a vertical line.
condition: Equation written as x = k or reducible to x = k.
response: State that the graph is a vertical line at x = k.
label: Principle / Model
example: $2x=3 \Rightarrow x=\frac{3}{2}$, which is a vertical line at $x=\frac{3}{2}$.

title: Identify y-Intercept from Graph
description: Determine the y-intercept by locating where the line crosses the y-axis.
condition: A graphed line crossing the y-axis.
response: State the y-value at x = 0.
label: Principle
example: If the line crosses the y-axis at $(0,-2)$, then the y-intercept is $-2$.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Interpret Slope as Rate of Change
description: Recognize that slope represents the rate of change (unit rate) in a proportional or linear relationship.
condition: Given a linear relationship between two quantities (e.g., feet per second).
response: State that the slope equals the unit rate (change in y per 1 unit of x).
label: Principle / Model
example: If a puck rises 4 feet per second, then slope = 4 because height increases 4 feet for each 1 second.

title: Interpret Slope in Context
description: Explain the meaning of the slope as the rate of change in a real-world situation.
condition: Given a linear model y = mx + b describing a contextual situation.
response: State that m represents the change in y for each 1-unit increase in x, interpreted with units.
label: Principle / Model
example: In $y = 0.3x + 27$, 0.3 means the coral reef grows 0.3 inches per year.

title: Match Graph to Linear Equation
description: Identify which equation corresponds to a graphed line by comparing slope and intercept.
condition: Graph of a line and multiple equation choices.
response: Select equation with matching slope and intercept.
label: Production / Skill
example: A line with slope $\frac{3}{2}$ and y-intercept -2 matches $y=\frac{3}{2}x-2$.
notes: Often based on visual slope recognition. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Perimeter of Equilateral Triangle
description: Compute the perimeter of an equilateral triangle from one side length.
condition: An equilateral triangle has side length s.
response: Compute perimeter as 3s.
label: Principle / Model

title: Perimeter of Rectangle
description: Compute the perimeter of a rectangle from its side lengths.
condition: A rectangle has two side lengths given (length and width).
response: Compute perimeter as 2l + 2w.
label: Principle / Model
example: If sides are $4x-2$ and $5x-5$, perimeter is $2(4x-2)+2(5x-5)$.

title: Plot Ordered Pair on Coordinate Plane
description: Plot a point on the coordinate plane given its ordered pair.
condition: Given an ordered pair (x, y).
response: Locate x on the horizontal axis and y on the vertical axis and mark the point.
label: Production / Skill
example: To graph $(1,-0.5)$, move 1 unit right and 0.5 units down.

title: Recognize Contradictory Equation
description: Identify when simplifying a linear equation results in a false statement.
condition: After simplifying, the equation becomes a false numerical statement (e.g., a = b where a ≠ b).
response: Conclude the equation has no solution.
label: Concept

title: Recognize Identity Equation
description: Identify when two expressions are equivalent for all values of the variable.
condition: After simplifying both sides of an equation, the variable terms and constants match exactly.
response: Conclude the equation has infinitely many solutions.
label: Concept
example: $4(x+9)=4x+36$ simplifies to $4x+36=4x+36$, so infinitely many solutions.

title: Recognize Similar Triangles
description: Identify triangles as similar when corresponding angles are equal and side lengths are proportional.
condition: Given two triangles with equal corresponding angles and proportional side lengths.
response: Conclude that the triangles are similar.
label: Concept
example: If two slope triangles on the same line have equal angle measures and proportional sides, then they are similar.

title: Recognize Slope-Intercept Form
description: Identify slope and y-intercept directly from an equation written in y = mx + b form.
condition: Equation written in the form y = mx + b.
response: State slope as m and y-intercept as b.
label: Rule
example: In $y=\tfrac{4}{3}x-2$, slope is $\tfrac{4}{3}$ and y-intercept is $-2$.
notes: Rationale marked No because this is based on definition of form rather than derivation.???

title: Rewrite Point-Slope Form
description: Convert an equation from point-slope form to slope-intercept form.
condition: Equation in the form y - y_1 = m(x - x_1).
response: Distribute m and solve for y to obtain y = mx + b.
label: Principle

title: Select Equivalent Linear Equations
description: Determine whether different equations represent the same line by checking algebraic equivalence.
condition: Multiple linear equations given as answer choices.
response: Rewrite each into slope-intercept form or simplify; select those with identical slope and intercept.
label: Principle
example: Given $y=\tfrac{4}{3}x-2$ and $y-2=\tfrac{4}{3}(x-3)$, rewrite second: $y=\tfrac{4}{3}x-4+2=\tfrac{4}{3}x-2$; they are equivalent.

title: Set Corresponding/Alternate Interior Angles Equal
description: When parallel lines are cut by a transversal, corresponding or alternate interior angles are congruent.
condition: Two parallel lines cut by a transversal with identified corresponding or alternate interior angles.
response: Set the angle measures equal in an equation.
label: Principle / Model
example: If $\angle1$ and $\angle2$ are corresponding, then $m\angle1 = m\angle2$.

title: Set Perimeters Equal
description: Form an equation by equating two perimeter expressions.
condition: Two shapes are stated to have the same perimeter.
response: Write an equation setting their perimeter expressions equal.
label: Principle / Model

title: Set Up Proportion from Similar Triangles
description: Use corresponding sides of similar triangles to form a proportion.
condition: Two triangles are similar and side lengths are labeled.
response: Write ratio of corresponding sides equal.
label: Principle / Model
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Solve Linear Equation with Variable on Both Sides
description: Rearrange and combine terms to isolate the variable when it appears on both sides of the equation.
condition: A linear equation with variable terms on both sides (e.g., $ax + b = cx + d$).
response: Move variable terms to one side, constants to the other, then solve for the variable.
label: Principle / Model

title: Solve Multi-Step Linear Equation
description: Isolate the variable in a linear equation using inverse operations after simplifying.
condition: A linear equation in one variable with variable terms on both sides simplifies to ax = b.
response: Perform inverse operations (add/subtract, divide) to solve for x.
label: Principle / Model

title: Solve One-Step Linear Equation
description: Isolate the variable using inverse operations.
condition: Equation of form ax = b.
response: Divide both sides by a.
label: Rule / Plan

title: Solve One-Step Multiplication Equation
description: Isolate a variable multiplied by a coefficient by dividing both sides by that coefficient.
condition: An equation of the form $ax = b$, where $a \neq 0$.
response: Divide both sides by $a$ to obtain $x = \frac{b}{a}$.
label: Principle / Model

title: Subtract Unit Rates to Compare
description: Find the difference between two linear rates by subtracting their unit rates.
condition: Given two unit rates describing the same quantity in the same units.
response: Subtract one rate from the other to find the difference.
label: Principle / Model
example: Air fryer: 12 min/lb; oven: 15.5 min/lb; difference = 15.5 - 12 = 3.5 minutes.

title: Translate Verbal Statement to Linear Expression
description: Convert phrases like '20 less than twice a number' into algebraic expressions.
condition: A verbal phrase describing operations on an unknown quantity.
response: Write a linear algebraic expression representing the phrase.
label: Rule / Plan

title: Use Distance = Rate × Time
description: Model motion problems using the formula distance equals rate times time.
condition: A constant speed and travel time are given or implied.
response: Write distance as d = rt.
label: Principle / Model

title: Use Proportional Relationship for Constant Rate
description: Model a proportional relationship with equation y = kx using constant rate of change.
condition: Table or graph shows proportional relationship through origin.
response: Determine constant of proportionality k and write y = kx.
label: Principle / Model

title: Verify Ordered Pair as Solution
description: Determine whether a given ordered pair satisfies a linear equation by substitution and comparison.
condition: Given a linear equation in two variables and a specific ordered pair.
response: Substitute the values and conclude whether the resulting statement is true or false.
label: Principle / Model

title: Write Equation from Constant of Proportionality
description: Express a proportional relationship as y = kx using the constant of proportionality.
condition: Given a proportional relationship with constant k.
response: Write equation in the form y = kx.
label: Principle / Model
example: If k = 15.5 minutes per pound, then y = 15.5x.

title: Write Equation Using Point-Slope Form
description: Write the equation of a line given a slope and a point using point-slope form.
condition: Given slope m and point (x₁, y₁).
response: Write equation as $y - y_1 = m(x - x_1)$.
label: Principle / Model

title: Write Slope-Intercept Equation from Context
description: Write a linear equation in slope-intercept form using an initial value and constant rate of change from a word problem.
condition: Given an initial value and a constant rate of change in a context.
response: Write equation in form y = mx + b with correct sign and units.
label: Principle / Model
